title: 1) Aging factors6
text:
Intrinsic aging factors
Ethnicity is the most important aging factor. Differences in pigmentation decide the color
of the face and affect the aging process. High levels of pigmentation are protective with
regard to the cumulative effects of photo-aging. Anatomical variations like skin thickness,
lipid composition, and skin rigidity are also intrinsic aging factors. Also, hormonal changes
such as in the estrogen level during menopause are one of the intrinsic aging factors.
Extrinsic aging factors
High temperature is the most important extrinsic aging factor. Smoking and exposure to
UV light are important  as well. Smoking  causes skin damage primarily by decreasing
capillary blood ﬂow to the skin. The effects of sunlight on the skin are profound, and are
estimated to account for up to 90% of visible skin aging.
